title: MLS credential identity not cross-validated against relay Ed25519 pub_id — credential spoofing possible
description: mls.rs MlsClient::new() sets the MLS BasicCredential to pub_id.as_bytes() — the pub_id the caller provides, which is the hex SHA-256 of the Ed25519 verifying key. The relay enforces that the wire-protocol pub_id matches the Ed25519 key during auth. But when a user sends a KeyPackage to another user (via PUBLISH_KEY_PACKAGE / GET_KEY_PACKAGE), the KeyPackage contains the MLS credential bytes — and those bytes are never cross-checked against the relay-authenticated pub_id. A malicious client could craft a KeyPackage with a spoofed pub_id in the credential (e.g. setting credential to some other user's pub_id). The admin calls add_member_to_group using that KeyPackage. The resulting group has a member whose MLS credential says they are user X, but they are actually user Y. Subsequent process_for_group calls would return sender_pub_id = X for messages actually sent by Y.
